posture: Quiet midweek scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the week's strongest 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the §1a–§1d free sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Several recurring or undated items were considered and excluded: the NSW staged FOGO mandate for businesses/institutions commences today (1 July 2026) for the largest generators, but this is a regulatory-rollout milestone rather than a fresh innovation signal and traces to earlier EPA/sector coverage, so it was not logged; ongoing NSW AI-in-planning activity (the ~$5.6m AI trial across 16 councils, DAISY at Wingecarribee) produced no new dated development today; the ACT multi-unit-dwelling FOGO pilot expansion (Belconnen/Tuggeranong) and Melbourne's Nokia Scene Analytics laneway-waste trial both trace to older/undated coverage; and recycled-content road/footpath trials (Wyndham, Hume, City of Sydney, SA council network) surfaced only via earlier or undated material. Today is a Wednesday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
